Empires and Puzzles Caitlin Review: The Enlightened Slayer (Base Hero)

Caitlin was once a unique force, offering a rare 'Dispel First' mechanic that terrified Taunt users. But with the Slayers family aging and stats skyrocketing, can 'The Enlightened' still shine, or is she just a flicker in the dark? We break down her viability in the modern meta.
Caitlin Review: Still The Queen of Dispel?
Caitlin: The "Dispel First, Ask Questions Later" Slayer
family, released back in the simpler times of 2021. In an era dominated by Taunt heroes like Krampus and Ludwig, Caitlin arrived with a very specific, very powerful promise: she clears the way
she strikes. While her Slayers family peers were focused on brute force, Caitlin was the tactical scalpel. However, time (and power creep) waits for no slayer. This review focuses on her
, analyzing if her unique utility can still hold up against modern behemoths with double her stats.

Caitlin’s design screams "battle-hardened inquisitor." She’s not your typical shiny paladin; she’s sporting rugged, practical armor and wields a weapon that looks like it doubles as a giant can opener for heavy plate. The art style of the Slayers family is distinct—gritty, slightly darker, and less "high fantasy" than Season 1 heroes. It’s a great look, though her pose suggests she’s just finished a long shift at the 'Dispel & Destroy' factory and is ready for a break.

This is the Slayers' gimmick. She speeds up (Mana Gen) and heals (HP Regen) every turn she survives or fires.
The speed. "Slayer" speed starts slow. In a game where Rush and Fast wars dominate, Caitlin takes too long to get going. By the time she reaches Fast speed (10 stacks), the battle is usually decided.

Upon release, Caitlin was a niche but respected counter to the "Taunt Meta." If you saw a Krampus or Black Knight, Caitlin was a safe yellow option to strip them naked without triggering their effects.
She has been crept hard. Heroes like
or even newer 4-stars do her job faster or with better stats. The introduction of her costume (reviewed separately) highlights just how far behind the base version is.

The base version is strictly for players who have no other "Dispel First" option or for very specific 5-star Rush Tournaments where her speed is fixed to Very Fast. Everywhere else, she is too slow and too squishy.
The costume adds ~400 points of Power. It's not a contest; the costume is a modern hero, the base is a relic.
. The costume does everything better, including keeping the "Dispel First" mechanic while adding Bleed and massive survivability.

If you pull Caitlin early, she is a great "Swiss Army Knife." Her ability to remove annoying defenses will help you clear Map Stages and early Raids. Don't worry about her speed; at lower levels, fights last long enough for her to shine.
hero. If you don't have her costume, she likely sits on your bench. Do not Limit Break the base version unless you have the costume and just want the stats for the costume side.
"Is Wizard class completely worthless for Caitlin? Since she dispels first... does that mean jinx wont activate?"
- A very common and valid complaint from the forums. The consensus: Yes, it's a design flaw.
"I almost immediately took her to +20... and I found that her hit is quite...weak."
- Players quickly realized she is a utility support, not a damage dealer.
"She’s the only one I did not manage to summon! But she sounds awesome to have!"
- Ah, the nostalgia of 2021 when she was a chase hero.

Caitlin is a hero with a fantastic skill design trapped in an outdated body. The "Dispel First" mechanic is timelessly useful, but without the stats to back it up, base Caitlin struggles to stay on the field. She is the definition of "Viable in Rush, Bench in Standard." If you have her costume, she becomes a goddess. If not, she’s a niche tool for when you absolutely, positively need to get rid of a Taunt.
A comprehensive review of Caitlin, the Holy 5-star hero from the Slayers of Fell Shadows family. Is her dispel-first mechanic enough to save her outdated stats in 2026?
